result.status_detail: perplexity.ai re-check: APF intercept partially functional — URL slug shows '/search/blocked-ce802fd0-022a-4563-a3c1-77fde15a48b7' (visible 'blocked' marker in thread URL = intercept reached request body). However, thread fetch returned 404 / redirected to home (perplexity.ai/?), so neither warning nor answer rendered to user. NEEDS_UUID_ECHO_INFRA classification confirmed — backend_uuid/context_uuid in response envelope likely don't match the client-side UUIDs, so client cannot find the thread to render.
result.intercept_evidence: URL slug contains literal 'blocked-' prefix — APF response envelope influenced thread routing
result.render_failure_evidence: Thread fetch 404 -> home redirect (no response/warning visible)
result.previous_classification: NEEDS_UUID_ECHO_INFRA — client requires backend_uuid/context_uuid echo from request body
result.test_progression: page_load=OK, login=OK, prompt_send=OK, intercept=PARTIAL (URL evidence), render=FAIL (404)
result.verdict_diff_from_previous: Same architectural issue confirmed — URL slug shows intercept reaches the request, but thread routing fails because envelope UUIDs don't echo client's request UUIDs

notes: Batch F-9 perplexity. URL evidence ('/search/blocked-' prefix in slug) confirms APF response envelope IS being used for routing decision but fails at thread retrieval. To fix: APF response must echo backend_uuid/context_uuid/uuid from the request body so client can find and render the blocked thread.
